description: Figure 16 presents a composition-phase map for Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels illustrating the precipitation of intragranular κ′-carbide (j0-carbide) as a function of alloy composition, with Al (wt%) on the horizontal axis and C (wt%) on the vertical axis. Open circles denote alloys in the single γ-austenite phase field, solid squares indicate the (γ + κ) two-phase field, and solid diamonds mark the (γ + α + κ) three-phase field. The solid lines labeled 15Mn, 20Mn, and 30Mn represent the κ-formation boundaries predicted by Eq. (3) from Huang et al., showing that higher Mn content shifts the boundary toward lower Al and C contents. Dashed and dash-dotted lines demarcate the γ + α phase boundaries for 15Mn and 30Mn alloys, respectively. The experimental data confirm that intragranular κ-carbide precipitation is favored at higher Al and C contents, while increasing Mn expands the γ + κ stability region. This figure supports the core conclusion that alloy composition can be designed to control κ-carbide precipitation and thereby tailor the microstructure and mechanical properties of austenitic lightweight steels.
